fields.label: Authentication Provider
fields.description: Specify which AWS credentials chain to use.

fields.label: Credentials Profile Name
fields.description: Credentials profile name, as specified in ~/.aws/credentials, leave blank for default.

fields.label: Assume Role ARN
fields.description: Optional. Specifying the ARN of a role will ensure that the selected authentication provider is used to assume the role rather than the credentials directly.

validations.message: When set, must be a valid IAM role ARN, e.g. arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/MyRole

fields.label: External ID
fields.description: If you are assuming a role in another account, that has been created with an external ID, specify the external ID here.

fields.label: Endpoint
fields.description: Optionally, specify a custom endpoint for the service

fields.label: Default Region
fields.description: Specify the region, such as for US West (Oregon) use ` us-west-2 ` as the region.

fields.label: Output Location
fields.description: Optional. If not specified, the default query result location from the Workgroup configuration will be used.

relationships.description: AWS access key pair — supplied together when authType is 'keys'
STS assume-role parameters — external ID is only meaningful when assumeRoleArn is set

instructions.msg: Six authentication values are stored in jsonData.authType. The five editor-selectable ones are: 'default' (AWS SDK Default — the default credential chain), 'keys' (Access & secret key — puts accessKey/secretKey in secureJsonData), 'credentials' (Credentials file — optionally provide jsonData.profile), 'ec2_iam_role' (Workspace IAM Role — no extra credentials needed), and 'grafana_assume_role' (Grafana Assume Role — Grafana Cloud only; visible only when the awsDatasourcesTempCredentials feature toggle is on). The sixth is 'arn' (deprecated legacy value; the backend maps it to 'default').

Minimal payload per auth method (jsonData.defaultRegion, jsonData.catalog, jsonData.database, and jsonData.workgroup are always required by the backend to actually run queries; the editor gates its 'Save & test' button until they are chosen). Access & secret key: {"jsonData":{"authType":"keys","defaultRegion":"us-east-1","catalog":"AwsDataCatalog","database":"default","workgroup":"primary"},"secureJsonData":{"accessKey":"<AKID>","secretKey":"<SECRET>"}}. Credentials file: {"jsonData":{"authType":"credentials","profile":"my-profile","defaultRegion":"us-east-1","catalog":"AwsDataCatalog","database":"default","workgroup":"primary"}}. AWS SDK Default / Workspace IAM Role / Grafana Assume Role: {"jsonData":{"authType":"default","defaultRegion":"us-east-1","catalog":"AwsDataCatalog","database":"default","workgroup":"primary"}} (swap authType to 'ec2_iam_role' or 'grafana_assume_role').

Assume role: setting jsonData.assumeRoleArn (with optional jsonData.externalId) makes the chosen provider assume the given IAM role via STS instead of using the credentials directly. This works with every auth type except 'grafana_assume_role' (which manages its own trust flow). For Grafana Assume Role, the assumeRoleArn and externalId fields disappear from the editor and Grafana derives the external ID from its namespace.

Session token: sessionToken is written to secureJsonData for temporary AWS STS credentials paired with 'keys' auth. The config editor does not render a UI field for it (the backend still reads it), so provisioning is the practical way to set it. Payload shape: {"secureJsonData":{"accessKey":"<AKID>","secretKey":"<SECRET>","sessionToken":"<STS-TOKEN>"}}.

secureJsonData values (accessKey, secretKey, sessionToken) are write-only: they can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read the secureJsonFields object (e.g. secureJsonFields.accessKey == true) instead of secureJsonData. Backend-only secrets like sessionToken never surface in secureJsonFields until they are set.

Storage-key casing pitfall: the frontend editor writes jsonData.catalog, jsonData.database, jsonData.workgroup, and jsonData.outputLocation (camelCase, lower initial letter), but the backend Go struct (pkg/athena/models/settings.go:26-29) declares those fields with `json:"Catalog"`, `json:"Database"`, `json:"WorkGroup"`, `json:"OutputLocation"` (PascalCase). Go's encoding/json accepts case-insensitive matches, so both spellings load correctly. Prefer the camelCase spelling on write; expect either on read from older provisioned configs.
